creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
That's why we have /usr/ports/UPDATING file.  It documents some of the problems users may encounter when upgrading ports.  We try our best to minimize these disruptions, but sometimes they are unavoidable.

You should get into the habit of checking this file for changes each time you update your ports collection and/or individual ports.  Particularly, see the entry for 20160829 which affects users of the nVidia driver.

> [...] though only by chance I had the old package still lying around.
nVidia driver port is written in a way that allows relatively easy custom builds by setting DISTVERSION and -DNO_CHECKSUM when invoking make(1).  This way you won't have to depend on previously built package(s) and can even try upcoming/beta versions.
